note: Labelled clarification-REPLY set for the WP9 one-round merge (parseClarificationReply). Each case pairs an ORIGINAL question that already has a committed intent fixture (tests/fixtures/llm/intent, replayed to build the PendingClarification) with a free-text REPLY, parsed merged with the pending clarification via tests/fixtures/llm/clarify. Mirrors benchmark/intent-labelled-set.json's calibration role and label-change policy: labels are product-policy judgments (the honest outcome for a given reply), not a knob to turn green — changing one is a reviewed decision recorded here, never a silent edit to make a red run pass. When a case fails because the clarify-mode prompt has a real gap, the prompt (CLARIFY_MODE_SECTION) is fixed with a rule and ALL clarify fixtures are re-recorded; when a case fails because the label itself was wrong, the case is corrected and the reasoning is added to this note. referenceDate matches the intent set so a shared reference clock resolves both turns identically. Cases: c-b15-full/c-b15-nomatch/c-b15-option-only exercise the B15 (bijstand, unmatched measure) clarification with a full resolving reply, a reply that still names an unmatched measure, and a reply that only supplies one of the two missing axes; c-b16-utrecht/c-b16-national exercise the B16 (huizenprijs in mijn buurt) clarification — a bare place name cannot resolve because average_existing_home_sale_price is national-only (region_on_national_measure), while 'het landelijke cijfer, voor 2024' resolves fully; c-utrecht-gemeente exercises the a-utrecht (benchmark/intent-labelled-set.json) region-kind ambiguity resolving to the B2 intent via 'de gemeente'. c-b15-smalltalk-abandon pins the 'reply abandons the clarification with smalltalk' shape (CLARIFY_MODE_SECTION's abandon rule): a reply that ignores the offered options and asks an unrelated meta-question about the product classifies on its own, same as a fresh smalltalk_or_other question, not as a further attempt to resolve bijstand. LABEL CORRECTION (WP14, 2026-07-04, reviewed): c-b15-option-only's axis pin dropped from ['period'] to none — the prompt-v4 model reads the present-tense original ('zitten') as period=latest per the standing present-tense policy (ADR 012 §4 / open-questions #40), so the old expectation of an open period axis pinned pre-v4 model detail, not policy; the model's honest doubt that bijstand ≡ werkloosheid (confidence 0.72 < 0.9 threshold) still forces a clarification, now on the measure axis. Both shapes are the same user-facing policy outcome — a clarification the respond layer converts to the final-round refusal-with-guidance — and THAT (outcome 'clarification', one compact question, no numbers) remains the pin. WP18 NOTE (2026-07-04): c-b15-smalltalk-abandon's label (parser outcome refusal/smalltalk) is unchanged; at the COMPOSED layer that reply now routes to the deterministic meta capabilities template (reason 'meta', ADR 022) because it genuinely asks what the system can do — pinned in tests/answer/respond-pipeline.test.ts, reviewed, not a silent edit.

cases.originalQuestion: Hoeveel mensen zitten in de bijstand?
cases.reply: het werkloosheidspercentage, voor heel Nederland, in het vierde kwartaal van 2025
